name: Andouille
recipeInstructions:
Andouille was a favorite in nineteenth-century New Orleans. This thick Cajun sausage is made with lean pork and pork fat and lots of garlic. Sliced about 1/2 inch thick and grilled, it makes a delightful appetizer. It is also used in a superb oyster and andouille gumbo popular in Laplace, a Cajun town about 30 miles from New Orleans that calls itself the Andouille Capital of the World.
1. Soak the casings for about an hour in cold water to soften them and loosen the salt.
2. Cut into 3-yard lengths.
3. Stuff one end of the casing with the sausage stuffer, placing the wide end against the sink faucet and running cold water through the inside to remove any remaining salt.
4. Roll up unused casings, place about 2 inches of coarse salt in a jar, lay the rolled casings on top, fill the rest of the jar with salt, close tightly, and refrigerate for later use.
5. Cut meat and fat into chunks about 1/2 inch across and pass through the coarse blade of the meat grinder.
6. Combine pork with remaining ingredients in a large bowl and mix well with a wooden spoon.
7. Cut casings into 26-inch lengths and stuff as follows:
- Tie knots in each casing piece about 2 inches from one end.
- Fit the open end over the sausage stuffer tip, sliding it to about 1 inch from the wide end.
- Push the rest of the casing onto the stuffer until the top touches the knot (creating accordion folds).
- Attach the stuffer to the meat grinder per instructions or hold the wide end against the opening by hand.
- Fill the hopper with stuffing and gradually feed into the machine if electric, using a wooden pestle for manual machines. The casing will fill and inflate slowly; stop about 1 1/4 inches from the funnel's end, remove the casing, smooth bumps carefully, and tie off the open end tightly or make a knot in the casing.
8. Repeat until all stuffing is used.
9. Cook by slicing 1/2 inch thick and grilling in a hot skillet with no water for about 12 minutes per side until brown and crisp at the edges.
Yield: Approximately 6 pounds of 20-inch sausage, 3 to 3-1/2 inches thick.
